brief_summary: Hero art for the Trends pillar piece on Cherry's brand-level pivot into magnetic/TMR switch tech via its XTRFY sub-brand (K5 Pro Compact launch): a 2px warm-grey sparkline that forks at a pivot node into a muted, dashed legacy continuation and a bold muted-terracotta ascent, with an open handoff ring partway along the ascent marking the sub-brand transfer from Cherry to XTRFY. A small abstract compact-keyboard silhouette sits below with one terracotta-glowing key, kept deliberately non-specific so the piece reads as trend analysis rather than a product spec sheet.
source: plan/bearings.md § Article hero art — colorful keyboard SVGs (locked 2026-05-09), trends piece → sparkline silhouette
template: directive: simple line drawing + single splash of color illustrative of subject
subject: Sparkline forking at a pivot node: a warm-grey historical climb arrives at the present decision point, then splits into a de-emphasized dashed legacy continuation and a bold muted-terracotta ascent. The ascent hands off partway through an open ring (the XTRFY sub-brand transfer) to a steeper, full-strength segment terminating at a focal anchor (the K5 Pro Compact launch). An abstract compact-keyboard outline below carries one terracotta-highlighted key as the keyboard-domain primitive required by the hero-art directive.
